title: Biomatter capture mechanism and method
abstract: In accordance with one embodiment, a method of capturing biomatter includes placing a device comprising the biomatter within an extension tube, the extension tube being coupled to a tray. A fold is created in the extension tube sealing the extension tube and the biomatter contained therein. The extension tube is secured to the tray to maintain the fold. The assembly is discarded, e.g., into a bio- waste disposal bag in a bio- waste container. The fold is a smooth fold, in contrast to a sharp tip, thus minimizing the possibility of puncture of the bio-waste disposal bag.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A biomatter capture mechanism comprising: an extension tube; and a tray, the extension tube being coupled to a distal end of the tray, the extension tube being foldable, wherein the extension tube is configured to be attached to the tray when folded. 2. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 1 further comprising a slot at a proximal end of the tray, the slot being configured to receive the extension tube therein. 3. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 2 wherein the extension tube is a cylindrical tube. 4. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 3 wherein the slot is curved. 5. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 4 wherein the slot tapers from the proximal end of the tray. 6. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 1 wherein the extension tube comprises a resin. 7. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 1 wherein the extension tube has a closed profile. 8. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 1 further comprising biomatter within the extension tube. 9. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 8 wherein the extension tube comprises a fold therein. 10. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 9 wherein the extension tube is snapped to the tray. 11. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 9 wherein the fold seals the extension tube. 12. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 1 further comprising: a tabbed end cap coupled to the extension tube, the tabbed end cap comprising a protruding tab; and an end cap slot within the tray configured to receive the protruding tab therein. 13. A biomatter capture mechanism comprising: a tray; an extension tube comprising a proximal end coupled to the tray; and an end cap coupled to a distal end of the extension tube, the end cap being configured to engage the tray. 14. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 13 wherein the end cap comprises end cap rails comprising clasps, the clasps being configured to engage end cap rails of the tray. 15. The biomatter capture mechanism of Claim 13 wherein the extension tube comprises a fold therein, the fold sealing the extension tube, and wherein the end cap is secured to the tray. 16. A method comprising: placing a device comprising biomatter within a cavity defined by an extension tube, the extension tube being coupled to a tray; creating a fold in the extension tube comprising sealing the extension tube and the biomatter therein; and coupling the extension tube to the tray to maintain the fold. 17. The method of Claim 16 wherein the coupling the extension tube to the tray comprises placing the extension tube within a tube slot at a proximal end of the tray. 18. The method of Claim 16 wherein the coupling the extension tube to the tray comprises placing an end cap coupled to the extension tube within an end cap slot at a bottom of the tray. 19. The method of Claim 16 wherein the coupling the extension tube to the tray comprises securing an end cap coupled to the extension tube to end cap rails protruding from the tray. 20. The method of Claim 16 further comprising removing the device from the cavity in a sterile condition prior to the placing.
